name: Eggplant-Stuffed Tomatoes "Pomodori Ripieni Di Melanzane
recipeIngredient: 1 eggplant (3/4 pound), peel/cut in half and dice
recipeInstructions:
NOTES:
If you prefer serving this dish warm, you can simply stuff the tomatoes with the hot eggplant and bake them for 5 minutes at 400°F. When the tomatoes are served at room temperature, they make a splendid antipasto; warm they are a great accompaniment to meat, poultry, or grilled fish.
1. Cut a lid from the top of each tomato; scoop out the flesh (reserve it for frittatas, sauces, or pizza), sprinkle the inside with salt, and turn upside-down to purge excess water for 30 minutes.
2. Heat olive oil in a wide pan; add garlic and eggplant, and sauté over medium heat until eggplant is golden but not brown, about 10 minutes, stirring often to prevent eggplant from sticking or burning; fold in scallion, capers, basil, and pepper.
3. Deglaze the pan with vinegar, season with salt, and stir well. Cook eggplant for another minute, or until vinegar has evaporated; remove from heat and cool to room temperature.
4. Stuff tomatoes with eggplant sauté and arrange on a serving platter; serve at room temperature. SERVES 6
Chardonnay Soave Classico California Sauvignon Blanc
